theory.canonical_statement: Chapter: The Gyro Helix Device: A Single Experiment Where Four Domains Converge Primary Registry IDs: 850 (Trawin Hamiltonian — the four domain Hamiltonian directly implements this), 020, 025 (winding number and topological charge conservation — SQUID and Quantum Zeno Effect) Secondary IDs: 002 (lemniscatic self intersection — Hopf fibration entanglement), 017–019 (Trawinistic operators — the device implements these in hardware) Tertiary IDs: 852–853 (cymatic field and Lyapunov stability — quantum acoustic transducers measure the cymatic field) The chapter's four component Hamiltonian $\hat{H} \text{total} = \hat{H} \text{mech} + \hat{H} \text{EM} + \hat{H} \text{quantum} + \hat{H} \text{topo}$ is a laboratory scale implementation of the Trawin Hamiltonian (ID 850):
theory.technical_interpretation: re Four Domains Converge* **Primary Registry IDs:** 850 (Trawin Hamiltonian — the four-domain Hamiltonian directly implements this), 020, 025 (winding number and topological charge conservation — SQUID and Quantum Zeno Effect) **Secondary IDs:** 002 (lemniscatic self-intersection — Hopf fibration entanglement), 017–019 (Trawinistic operators — the device implements these in hardware) **Tertiary IDs:** 852–853 (cymatic field and Lyapunov stability — quantum acoustic transducers measure the cymatic field) --- ## The Four-Domain Hamiltonian and ID 850 The chapter's four-component Hamiltonian $\hat{H}_\text{total} = \hat{H}_\text{mech} + \hat{H}_\text{EM} + \hat{H}_\text{quantum} + \hat{H}_\text{topo}$ is a laboratory-scale implementation of the Trawin Hamiltonian (ID 850): | Trawin Hamiltonian Component | Gyro-Helix Component | Physical Realisation | |---|---|---| | $\hat{H}_\text{tunnel}$ In technical terms, the entry packages the selected formula as the operative carrier, the nearby equations as the progression layer, and the symbol key as the compact interpretation of variables used by the source.
